ogTitle: Tynong North vs Tyntynder South: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tynong North and Tyntynder South.
heading: Tynong North vs Tyntynder South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tynong North, Victoria and Tyntynder South, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tynong North has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Tyntynder South (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tynong North or Tyntynder South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tynong North has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Tyntynder South scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tynong North scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Tyntynder South scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tynong North or Tyntynder South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tynong North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
